title: After lawsuit, Secretary of Commonwealth agrees to produce legally mandated report on voting in jail
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Secretary of Commonwealth William Galvin agrees to produce voting reports on jailed individuals after legal action.
The VOTES Act, signed in 2022, mandates post-election reports on eligible incarcerated voters in Massachusetts.
Galvin's office cited technical issues for failing to report on voting in jails for 2024 and 2025 elections.

title: New Patriots fullback Reggie Gilliam sees a similarity between Drake Maye and former MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Reggie Gilliam, former Buffalo Bills fullback, joins the New England Patriots on a three-year contract.
Gilliam compares new Patriots quarterback Drake Maye to Buffalo Bills' Josh Allen, highlighting their similar build and potential.
Gilliam aims to enhance the Patriots' running game, drawing on his experience with Buffalo's NFL-leading rush offense.

title: Red Sox infielder Romy Gonzalez undergoes shoulder surgery; Boston signs lefty reliever ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Romy Gonzalez will miss at least two months of the 2026 season after shoulder surgery.
Gonzalez's procedure was an arthroscopic debridement to address shoulder issues from the 2025 season.
The Red Sox signed lefty reliever Danny Coulombe to a one-year contract to strengthen their bullpen.

title: High school student allegedly killed Danvers woman in her home in ‘random’ attack, DA says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: High school senior Anthony DeMayo charged with murder after Janet Swallow, 68, was found dead in Danvers home.
DeMayo, from Lynn, was found with a knife, alleged to be blood-stained, on Standish Street by police.
Prosecutors say there's no known connection between DeMayo and Swallow, describing the incident as a random act.

title: Boston police seeking suspect who allegedly assaulted pregnant woman in Roxbury laundromat
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police are searching for a suspect who allegedly assaulted a pregnant woman at a Roxbury laundromat on March 1.
The incident reportedly began over a payment card issue at 104 Warren St., escalating when the suspect kicked the victim.
The suspect is described as a 6’0” light-skinned male in a Yankees hat; witnesses can provide tips to CrimeStoppers.

title: Fired Mass. cop arrested in N.H. for allegedly impersonating a police officer
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mikel Beltran, 37, was arrested in Londonderry, N.H. for allegedly impersonating a police officer by flashing blue lights.
Beltran was fired from Lawrence Police in March 2025 and had his certification suspended by the Massachusetts Peace Officer Standards and Training Commission.
Beltran is suing Lawrence police, claiming unconstitutional phone searches led to his firing for alleged misconduct.

title: NFL insider believes Eagles will lower asking price to Patriots in A.J. Brown trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Philadelphia Eagles are asking for a 2027 first-round pick and a 2026 second-round pick for A.J. Brown.
Mike Giardi believes the Eagles will lower their asking price, making a trade to the Patriots more likely.
The Eagles face over $43 million in dead cap if trading Brown before June 1, but only $16.4 million afterward.

title: Truck catches fire on Mass. Pike during early-morning commute, causing lane shutdowns
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A truck caught fire Thursday morning on the Mass. Pike in Westborough, closing all westbound lanes temporarily.
Firefighters from Westborough and Grafton responded around 8 a.m., extinguishing the blaze by 8:30 a.m.
All lanes were reopened by 1:30 p.m.; no injuries were reported, and the fire's cause remains under investigation.

title: New Patriots WR Romeo Doubs explains why he chose New England in free agency
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Romeo Doubs signs a four-year contract with the New England Patriots worth up to $80 million.
Doubs chose New England for the opportunity to work with quarterback Drake Maye and a strong coaching staff.
Doubs previously played for Green Bay, recording 55 catches, 724 yards, and six touchdowns in 2025.

title: SUV owner experiences a serious – and not uncommon – engine issue
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2018 Hyundai Tucson owner experienced engine failure, requiring a full engine replacement under the 100,000-mile warranty.
Hyundai, Kia, Toyota, and Chevrolet face similar engine issues due to manufacturing defects causing power loss and potential failure.
Hyundai and Kia models often suffer from improperly machined bearings leading to engine knock and limp mode activation.
